title: crotalaria ukingensis
intro: Crotalaria ukingensis is a species of flowering plant in the family Fabaceae, commonly known as a legume or pea family. It is native to East Africa, particularly in Kenya, and has been used for centuries in traditional medicine due to its medicinal properties. The plant has been found to have anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties, which contribute to its health benefits, including the treatment of wounds, fever, and respiratory issues. In herbal preparations, Crotalaria ukingensis is often used in the form of a decoction or infusion, where the dried leaves and stems are steeped in hot water to create a medicinal tea.
